denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION
 Date of Notice: 03/29/2026
UnitedHealthcare Choice Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100514735  Group #: GRP-1065
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-112926
Claim #: CLM-2026-191446
Date of Service (requested): 03/09/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Northline Medical Group
 Dear Plan Member,
 This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination regarding prior authorization. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 03/11/2026 for Eculizumab (Soliris) related to Myasthenia gravis, generalized (G70.00). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request. CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Eculizumab (Soliris)
Diagnosis (as submitted): Myasthenia gravis, generalized (G70.00)
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service. Clinical policy applied: CDG-UM125 under UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Guideline (CDG) and applicable Medical Policy Bulletin
 EXPLANATION OF DECISION
The requested service is not medically necessary because it does not meet the plan's medical necessity criteria.
Our review applied the criteria in CDG-UM125 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not meet medical necessity criteria because the service is not medically necessary for this member at this time. Eculizumab (Soliris) is non-preferred; a formulary alternative must be tried for Myasthenia gravis, generalized (G70.00). A lower-tier agent is available without demonstrated failure of the preferred option. Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with formulary tier dispute. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, including plain-language descriptions, as required by applicable federal standards. If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials. Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a UnitedHealthcare medical director. Peer-to-peer review must be requested before a formal appeal is initiated for the same authorization decision. YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Many members use this information when preparing an appeal. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials. APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination through our internal appeals process. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100514735) and reference PA-2026-112926. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination. Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above. Sincerely,
 UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
Clinical Operations
clinical_context: The 57-year-old member has Myasthenia gravis, generalized (G70.00). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Eculizumab (Soliris) recommended as the next appropriate step. Positive acetylcholine receptor antibodies; failed pyridostigmine and steroids. The prior-authorization packet included dated clinic notes, medication trials, and objective findings supporting medical necessity. The denial rationale does not reference those submissions. Treating clinicians document ongoing functional impairment and recommend proceeding without further delay when benefit-plan criteria have been met based on the record on file.
